findings.description: Professional engineer seal/stamp image is missing in the HTML conversion
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 shows a North Carolina Professional Engineer seal (SEAL 038141) with DocuSigned signature. The HTML version on page 2 shows only the seal image without proper rendering context.

findings.description: Cover page layout not preserved - title and content layout differs significantly
findings.evidence: PDF has a centered, large-font title block with horizontal rule separator. HTML renders as a more linear layout without the same visual hierarchy of the cover page.

findings.description: Geosyntec logo appears differently rendered between PDF and HTML
findings.evidence: PDF shows the Geosyntec logo prominently at top of cover page. HTML includes it but with different sizing/placement.

findings.description: Table of Contents layout compressed - PDF has clear separation between TOC, List of Tables, List of Figures sections
findings.evidence: PDF page 3 shows well-separated sections with clear visual hierarchy. HTML merges these into a continuous flow without the same page-level separation.

findings.description: Figure 1 (Outfall 002 Sampling Locations map) - aerial imagery/map is present but the inset detail box with red border may not render properly
findings.evidence: PDF shows a detailed satellite map with inset showing sampling locations. HTML includes the image but the detail and legibility may be affected.

findings.description: Figure 2 flowchart for determining concentrations at Outfall 002 - complex flowchart with decision diamonds and result boxes
findings.evidence: PDF shows a detailed flowchart with colored decision diamonds (blue) and result boxes (green). HTML text version loses the visual flowchart structure, rendering it as a flat text list of YES/NO decisions.

findings.description: Figure 3 flowchart for determining concentrations at River Water Intake - flowchart rendered as flat text
findings.evidence: PDF shows a visual flowchart with decision diamond and result boxes. HTML renders this as sequential text (YES, NO, labels) losing the visual decision-tree structure.

findings.description: Page footer information (page numbers, document title, date) intermixed with body content in HTML
findings.evidence: HTML shows 'Outfall 002 Mass Load 2 August 2020' and similar footer text inline between content sections, whereas PDF keeps these as separate page footers.

findings.description: DocuSign Envelope ID and Geosyntec header repeated inline within body content in HTML
findings.evidence: HTML shows 'DocuSign Envelope ID: 1B477D8C-F5AD-4BCC-B766-EF28605DC046' and Geosyntec header blocks between content paragraphs, whereas PDF has these as unobtrusive page headers.

findings.description: Multiple h1 tags used in the HTML. The document title uses h1, and then 'TABLES' and 'FIGURES' section divider pages also use h1. The document should have only one h1.
findings.evidence: HTML has h1 for 'SITE CONVEYANCE NETWORK AND OUTFALL 002 PFAS MASS LOADING CALCULATION PROTOCOL', h1 for 'TABLES', and h1 for 'FIGURES'. Only the document title should be h1.

findings.description: Heading hierarchy is inconsistent. Section 1 'INTRODUCTION' uses h2, Section 2 'OUTFALL 002 AND RIVER INTAKE SAMPLING' uses h2, but subsections like '2.1 Outfall 002 Sample Types' use h3, which is correct. However, 'TABLE OF CONTENTS', 'LIST OF TABLES', 'LIST OF FIGURES', and 'LIST OF ABBREVIATIONS' all use h3, which is inconsistent with their role as standalone headings.
findings.evidence: TABLE OF CONTENTS, LIST OF TABLES, LIST OF FIGURES, LIST OF ABBREVIATIONS are h3 in the HTML but are standalone section headers in the PDF.

findings.description: In Table 1, all rows show 'Table 3+ Lab SOP' in the Analytical Method column. In the PDF, this value appears once as a merged/spanning cell covering all rows. The repetition is technically accurate but represents a structural difference.
findings.evidence: PDF shows 'Table 3+ Lab SOP' as a single merged cell spanning all analyte rows. HTML repeats it in each row.

findings.description: In Equation 2, the HTML uses 'C' (uppercase) for the individual compound concentration variable c_OF002,j,i, while the PDF uses lowercase 'c'. This is inconsistent within the HTML itself - the first form of Equation 2 uses uppercase C correctly, but the expanded form also uses uppercase C when the PDF clearly uses lowercase 'c'.
findings.evidence: PDF: 'c𝑂𝑂𝑂𝑂002,j,i = is the measured or estimated concentration of the ith PFAS compound'. HTML: 'C<sub>OF002,j,i</sub> = is the measured or estimated concentration of the i<sup>th</sup> PFAS compound'. The variable definitions in the PDF use lowercase c for individual compound concentrations vs uppercase C for total concentration.

findings.description: The page headers containing the DocuSign Envelope ID and Geosyntec logo are repeated for every page in the HTML, creating visual duplication. While these are present on each page of the PDF, typical HTML conversion should consolidate repeated headers.
findings.evidence: DocuSign Envelope ID and Geosyntec logo image tags appear repeatedly throughout the HTML for each page break.
